2606.24004.title: Towards Spec Learning: Inference-Time Alignment from Preference Pairs
2606.24004.paper_summary: Steering a large language model (LLM) toward a desired behavior typically relies on an iterative process of hand-crafting a prompt based on a careful inspection of the model's responses. This is an involved, brittle, and error-prone process. Preference-based fine-tuning is a more rigorous but often prohibitively expensive solution. We propose spec learning, a framework that relies on a brief user instruction and a small set of preference judgments. These are compiled into specifications in the form of natural-language prompts for an LLM. Specifications condition LLMs at inference time, and no parameter updates to the underlying models are required. We show that the responses generated based on the compiled specifications often outperform direct preference optimization (DPO) on datasets from specialized domains whose preference signal is dense. Unlike opaque weight updates, the resulting specifications are human-readable and double as interpretable and transparent written embodiments of the preference signal that produced them.
2606.24004.paper_summary_zh: 引導大型語言模型（LLM）朝向期望的行為通常依賴於一個迭代過程，該過程基於對模型回應的仔細檢查來手工製作提示。這是一個複雜、脆弱且容易出錯的過程。基於偏好的微調是一種更嚴謹但通常代價高昂的解決方案。我們提出了規範學習（spec learning），這是一個依賴於簡短用戶指令和一小組偏好判斷的框架。這些被編譯成自然語言提示的規範，用於LLM。規範在推理時對LLM進行條件設定，並且不需要對基礎模型進行參數更新。我們展示了基於編譯規範生成的回應，通常在來自偏好信號密集的專業領域數據集上表現超過直接偏好優化（DPO）。與不透明的權重更新不同，所產生的規範是人類可讀的，並且同時作為可解釋和透明的書面體現，反映了生成它們的偏好信號。
2606.24004.author: Dhriti Krishnan et.al.
2606.24004.authors: Dhriti Krishnan, Tejas Goyal, Jaromir Savelka
